rawCommits.subject: Fix update banner stacking and Windows auto-update sidecar lock
rawCommits.body:
The update banner was falling back to a transparent background because it
referenced an undefined --color-surface-raised variable, and its z-index
of 1200 let BottomTaskbar (9999) and other overlays paint over it. Switch
to --color-surface and raise z-index to 10001 so the banner is always on
top with an opaque surface.

On Windows, auto-update also failed to apply because the NSIS installer
kills aura.exe but leaves its sidecar children (aura-node.exe local
harness, managed Vite dev server) running. Those children hold file
locks in the install directory, so the installer cannot overwrite
binaries. Register a shutdown hook on UpdateState that posts a new
ShutdownForUpdate event to the main event loop; the handler stops the
sidecars and exits, and install_and_restart then terminates the process
so the installer can finish replacing files.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Wire browser backend events and add Chromium CDP backend
rawCommits.body:
The sidekick browser shipped with a working control plane but no way
for backend events to reach the WebSocket. This fixes the plumbing
and adds the real Chromium backend so frames and navigation state
can actually flow end-to-end.

Event plumbing
- SessionHandle no longer owns the event receiver or cancel token, and
  its Drop impl no longer cancels the session. Previously the REST
  spawn handler dropped the handle to return JSON, which cancelled
  the session before the WS could ever connect.
- Events receiver now lives in the manager's RegistryEntry; new
  BrowserManager::take_events and project_id_of let the WS handler
  pick up the stream and bind nav records to the right project.
- WS handler pumps ServerEvent::Frame, Nav, and Exit to the client,
  recording visits for both client-initiated navigates and
  server-pushed navigation state.

Chromium backend (cdp feature)
- New crates/aura-os-browser/src/cdp_backend.rs uses chromiumoxide to
  launch a single shared headless Chromium, spawn a fresh Page per
  session, stream JPEG screencast frames, and translate every
  ClientMsg variant (Navigate / Back / Forward / Reload / Resize /
  Mouse / Wheel / Key) into CDP calls.
- Per-session task select!s over screencast frames, frameNavigated
  + loadEventFired events, the session command channel, and the
  cancel token, emitting ServerEvent::Frame / Nav / Exit.
- Gated behind the cdp cargo feature so base builds stay lean.

Server integration
- aura-os-server gains a browser-cdp feature that forwards to
  aura-os-browser/cdp. build_app_state picks CdpBackend when enabled
  and falls back to StubBackend otherwise, logging which backend is
  active so operators know whether rendering is wired up.

Tests
- New manager tests for take_events and project_id_of round trips.
- New CDP backend unit tests for mouse button mapping and the
  base64 sniff heuristic.
- 37 browser-crate tests pass; full workspace cargo check is clean
  in both default and browser-cdp configurations.

rawCommits.subject: Phase 3: host super-agent sessions end-to-end on aura-harness
rawCommits.body:
Closes out the super-agent / harness unification plan by giving
aura-os-server everything it needs to boot a harness-hosted
super-agent from a SuperAgentProfile, and by teaching the wire
protocol to carry the intent-classifier spec the harness uses to
filter per-turn tool surfaces.

Protocol (crates/aura-protocol)
- Add `IntentClassifierSpec` + `IntentClassifierRule` and embed
  `intent_classifier: Option<IntentClassifierSpec>` on `SessionInit`.
  The shape matches what `aura-tools::IntentClassifier::from_profile_json`
  already deserializes, plus a `tool_domains` map so the harness can
  resolve each opaque ToolDefinition back to its domain without a copy
  of the aura-os enum. Backward compatible: field is optional and
  `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`.

aura-os-super-agent-profile
- Add `tier1_domains_snake_case`, `classifier_rules_snake_case`, and
  `tool_domains_snake_case` so callers can render the profile directly
  into the wire spec without re-implementing the domain->string mapping.

aura-os-super-agent
- New `harness_handoff` module converts a `SuperAgentProfile` into:
  * `Vec<InstalledTool>` pointing at a single dispatcher endpoint
    (one InstalledTool per manifest entry, auth carried as a dynamic
    JWT injection so tokens never get cached on the harness),
  * `IntentClassifierSpec` for per-turn filtering, and
  * a fully-formed `SessionInit` combining system prompt, tools, and
    classifier.
- Depends on aura-protocol directly (profile stays pure data).

aura-os-server
- `POST /api/super_agent/tools/:name` dispatcher
  (`handlers/super_agent_tools::dispatch_super_agent_tool`): resolves
  a tool by name from `SuperAgentService::tool_registry`, builds a
  `SuperAgentContext` from JWT + `X-Aura-Org-Id`, and executes in
  process. This is the single HTTP surface every
  profile-manifest-derived InstalledTool targets, which keeps the
  harness side generic.
- `HarnessSuperAgentDriver` in `harness_super_agent_driver.rs` drives
  a harness session end-to-end: it assembles the `SessionInit` from
  `build_super_agent_session_init`, opens the WS stream via
  `HarnessClient`, forwards the user message, and relays harness
  `OutboundMessage`s back to the caller. Ships with `preview_*`
  helpers so upstream code can inspect the payload without opening a
  socket, and is not yet wired to production routes.
- Router registers the new route; lib.rs re-exports the driver types.

aura-os-link
- Fill in `intent_classifier: None` on existing `SessionInit` literals
  in the swarm and local harness paths so they keep compiling with
  the new field.

Tests
- `apps/aura-os-server/tests/harness_super_agent_driver_test.rs`:
  mock axum+WS harness validates that the driver ships the right
  `SessionInit` (prompt mentions org, installed tool count matches
  profile manifest, intent_classifier populated) and that harness
  OutboundMessages get forwarded. Second case covers init rejection.
- `harness_handoff` tests cover dispatcher-URL shape, streaming
  flag propagation, org-scoped prompt, classifier spec parity with
  the profile, and deterministic payloads.
- `aura-os-super-agent-profile` gains unit tests for the new
  snake_case renderers.

Nothing in the legacy in-process super-agent path changes yet; the
harness driver and dispatcher are additive and opt-in.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: Phase 4: route super-agent chat through harness driver (opt-in)
rawCommits.body:
Adds a second execution path for super-agents that delegates the LLM
loop to an aura-harness node via the HarnessSuperAgentDriver shipped
in phase 3, while keeping the in-process SuperAgentStream as the
default. An individual agent opts in by tagging itself
`host_mode:harness`; operators can force every super-agent onto
the new path via `AURA_SUPER_AGENT_HOST_MODE=harness`.

End-to-end wiring:

- `apps/aura-os-server/src/harness_super_agent_driver.rs`
  - Adds `HarnessSuperAgentDriver::start_with_init` so callers can
    submit a fully-populated SessionInit (conversation history,
    aura_agent_id, aura_session_id) instead of re-running
    `build_super_agent_session_init` unchanged; plumbs attachments
    through on the opening UserMessage. `start` now delegates to
    `start_with_init` so no caller breaks.

- `apps/aura-os-server/src/handlers/agents/super_agent_harness.rs`
  (new)
  - `HostMode` + `host_mode_for_agent` gate reading the
    `host_mode:harness` agent tag and the `AURA_SUPER_AGENT_HOST_MODE`
    env override.
  - `handle_super_agent_via_harness` mirrors
    `handle_super_agent_stream`: registers the run in
    `state.super_agent_runs` (so reset / new_session cancels it
    mid-flight), wires the existing `spawn_chat_persist_task` onto
    a `broadcast::Sender<HarnessOutbound>`, and spawns a bridge
    task that forwards every `OutboundMessage` from the driver's
    mpsc receiver into that broadcast. Because
    `HarnessOutbound = aura_protocol::OutboundMessage` the bridge
    is a pure copy; no adapter is needed.
  - `forward_driver_error` surfaces `HarnessSuperAgentError` to
    the client as a `HarnessOutbound::Error` frame so startup
    failures (harness down, init rejected, auth) close the SSE
    stream cleanly instead of hanging.
  - The handler intentionally does **not** populate
    `AppState.super_agent_messages`: the harness writes its own
    transcript to the kernel record log, and the in-memory cache
    only exists to feed the Claude-shaped history back to
    `SuperAgentStream`. On cold start the dispatcher in chat.rs
    reconstructs `SessionInit.conversation_messages` from session
    events, matching what `handle_super_agent_stream` does when
    its cache misses.

- `apps/aura-os-server/src/handlers/agents/chat.rs`
  - `send_agent_event_stream` now picks between the in-process and
    harness paths via `host_mode_for_agent`.
  - New `dispatch_super_agent_via_harness` peer of
    `handle_super_agent_stream` resolves org, loads history via
    `session_events_to_conversation_history`, sets up the persist
    context, and calls the new handler.
  - `ChatPersistCtx::session_id()` accessor so the dispatcher can
    propagate the aura-os session id into
    `SessionInit.aura_session_id` for cross-system correlation.
  - `SSE_NO_BUFFERING_HEADERS` and `harness_broadcast_to_sse`
    are now `pub(crate)` so the new handler reuses the shared
    framing machinery verbatim.

- `apps/aura-os-server/src/handlers/agents/mod.rs` exposes the
  new `super_agent_harness` module crate-wide.

Tests (unit, in the new module):

- `default_host_mode_is_in_process` — empty tags + no env var
  keeps today's behaviour.
- `host_tag_opts_into_harness` +
  `host_tag_is_case_insensitive` — opt-in tag works.
- `env_override_flips_to_harness` +
  `env_override_other_value_stays_in_process` — fleet-wide flag
  honours `harness` only.
- `bridge_forwards_events_until_channel_closes` — every
  OutboundMessage arrives on the broadcast subscriber and the
  receiver closes when the driver mpsc drops.
- `bridge_exits_promptly_on_cancel` — cancellation drains the
  bridge even when the mpsc sender is still alive (proves reset /
  new_session can tear the turn down).

All 7 new tests pass; phase 3's
`harness_super_agent_driver_test` integration tests still pass;
no clippy warnings on the touched files. Pre-existing failures in
`handlers::agents::workspace_tools::tests::canonical_secret_*` and
`handlers::trusted_mcp::tests::*` were verified to reproduce
without these changes and are out of scope.

Backwards compatibility: this commit is strictly additive. Until
an agent is tagged or the env var is set, every super-agent turn
continues to run through `handle_super_agent_stream` exactly as
before. Phase 5 will layer the Local/Cloud interface toggle on top;
phase 6 flips the default and retires the in-process path.

Made-with: Cursor

Give users an explicit Local/Cloud choice when running a super-agent and
route both paths through the unified HarnessClient wiring so the toggle
round-trips through the `host_mode:harness` tag instead of a side
channel.

Backend
- dto.rs: add `tags: Option<Vec<String>>` to CreateAgentRequest and
  UpdateAgentRequest with docstring noting `None` means "leave tags
  untouched" for updates.
- aura-os-network types: forward `tags` on create/update requests
  (skip_serializing_if = None) and accept `tags` on NetworkAgent reads
  with `serde(default)` so older aura-network deployments still
  deserialize cleanly; agent_from_network now merges these back into
  the in-memory Agent.
- handlers/agents/crud.rs: plumb `body.tags` through create_agent and
  update_agent; vm_id persistence path sends `tags: None` to avoid
  clobbering.
- handlers/super_agent.rs: setup_super_agent leaves tags alone
  (tags: None) and a new `harness_health` handler calls
  HarnessClient::probe() so the UI can show a reachability pill.
- harness_client.rs: add HarnessProbeResult + probe() that hits
  GET /agents/:nil/head, measures latency, and folds transport/HTTP
  errors into a single struct; exported from lib.rs.
- router.rs: expose GET /api/super_agent/harness/health.
- agent_create_test.rs: fix struct literals for the new tags field.

Frontend
- api/agents.ts: accept optional `tags?: string[]` on create/update.
- useAgentEditorForm.ts: new `HostMode` state + HOST_MODE_HARNESS_TAG
  constant; hydrate from agent.tags, default "local" for new agents,

- AgentEditorModal.tsx / AgentEditorForm.tsx: render a "Run on this

rawCommits.subject: Fix AppsModal drag visuals: vertical-only, stable overlay, div-based rows
rawCommits.body:
The drag overlay was visually drifting (appearing far off to the side and
losing sight of the dragged item) for two reasons:

- No axis constraint, so horizontal pointer delta pulled the ghost off the
  list. Added @dnd-kit/modifiers with restrictToVerticalAxis so drag is
  locked to the up/down axis the sections live on.
- Rows were <li> inside <ul>, but the DragOverlay renders its child
  detached from that list at the document body. An orphaned list-item got
  default browser layout (marker area, margins) which pushed content
  sideways. Swapped to <div role="listitem"> in a <div role="list">.

Also stopped committing cross-section moves from onDragOver. Committing
mid-drag re-mounted the source row in the other section, which shifted the
activator's bounding rect under the pointer and caused the overlay to
jump. Cross-section and intra-section moves are now both resolved in a
single onDragEnd using the drop target's section and position, keeping
the DOM stable during the drag.

Minor: add box-sizing/width on .row so it fills the DragOverlay's fixed
width cleanly, and disable drop animation for an immediate snap.

rawCommits.subject: phase6: one-shot super-agent migrator + feature-gate legacy in-process stream
rawCommits.body:
Adds the safe half of Phase 6 of the super-agent / harness unification
plan (plans/unify_super_agents_into_harness_630aa7f8.plan.md). All
changes are additive and reversible:

- New super_agent_migration module: idempotent boot-time migrator that
  finds legacy super-agent records (role == "super_agent" or tag), adds
  host_mode:harness + preset:ceo, stamps migration:super_agent_v1, and
  best-effort pushes new tags upstream. Local shadow writes are the
  source of truth; network push is skipped silently when there is no
  cached JWT (typical on fresh boot). Respects a host_mode:in_process
  operator pin and the AURA_SUPER_AGENT_MIGRATE=off env rollback.
- Wired into server boot (main.rs) after build_app_state. Logs a
  MigrationReport and never panics.
- super-agent creation (handlers/super_agent.rs) now tags new records
  with super_agent, host_mode:harness, preset:ceo so freshly-created
  CEOs route through the harness by default.
- Adds legacy_super_agent_stream cargo feature (ON by default). When
  OFF, the HostMode::InProcess branch in agents/chat.rs returns an
  error instead of running the in-process SuperAgentStream.
  handle_super_agent_stream stays compiled behind a cfg_attr dead_code
  allow so --no-default-features builds are clean. Nothing is deleted
  --- SuperAgentStream, handle_super_agent_stream, and the
  aura-os-super-agent crate are untouched, per plan constraints.
- Documents the 3-step Phase 6 rollout in super_agent_harness.rs:
  migrate (feature ON) -> rebuild with feature OFF -> follow-up
  cleanup PR.

Tests (all passing under --test-threads=1):
- super_agent_migration: pure-helper unit tests (idempotent, honors
  operator pin, de-duplicates harness tag, role+tag match is
  case-insensitive) plus integration tests over a real build_app_state
  tempdir (selects only unmigrated legacy super-agents; second run
  reports already_migrated=2 migrated=0; AURA_SUPER_AGENT_MIGRATE=off
  produces skipped_via_env with zero mutations).
- super_agent_harness: two new phase6_* tests asserting host_mode
  routing (harness tag wins for super_agent, in_process pin stays on
  legacy path).

Verified: cargo build -p aura-os-server (default), with
--features legacy_super_agent_stream, and with --no-default-features
all compile clean. cargo clippy -p aura-os-server --lib --no-deps
reports no new warnings on the touched files.

rawCommits.subject: aura-protocol: SessionInit.agent_permissions + preset fields (additive)
rawCommits.body:
Phase 5 of the super-agent unification: let a SessionInit carry an
explicit permissions bundle or a named preset so the harness can enforce
scope + capability checks for cross-agent tools without coupling
aura-protocol to aura-core.

New wire types in crates/aura-protocol/src/lib.rs (mirror aura-core so
the protocol crate stays self-contained):
  * AgentPermissionsWire - { scope, capabilities }
  * AgentScopeWire       - { orgs, projects, agent_ids }

rawCommits.subject: Portal AppsModal DragOverlay to document.body so the ghost follows the cursor
rawCommits.body:
The ZUI Modal's .modal element keeps a `transform: scale(1)` applied after
its scale-in animation (animation-fill-mode: forwards). That turns the
modal into the containing block for every `position: fixed` descendant,
including the DragOverlay's ghost. As a result the ghost was positioned
relative to the modal's top-left corner instead of the viewport, which
made it render visibly offset from the cursor (often outside the modal's
clip box, where it appeared "hidden" during drag).

Render the DragOverlay via createPortal into document.body so it escapes
the transformed ancestor and `position: fixed` resolves against the
viewport again. Context still flows through the portal because the
DragOverlay stays a React child of the DndContext. Also give it an
explicit z-index (above the ZUI Modal's z-index: 50) so the ghost always
stacks on top of the modal backdrop and content.

rawCommits.subject: browser/terminal: per-user session ownership + project-scoped authz
rawCommits.body:
Previously `GET /api/browsers`, `DELETE /api/browsers/:id`, the ws upgrade,
and the terminal/browser spawn endpoints operated on the global registry
with no regard for which caller owned which session. On a multi-user
desktop this let one session see or kill another user's live browser.

- `BrowserManager` now tracks an `owner_id: Option<String>` per session.
  Add `spawn_for_owner`, `list_for_owner`, and `is_owned_by` helpers;
  keep the old `spawn` as an unowned variant for tests / local tooling.
- Every browser REST + ws handler now extracts the caller's
  `AuthSession` and scopes list/kill/ws to sessions the caller owns.
- Browser spawn / settings / detect and terminal spawn paths call
  `ensure_project_access` against `aura-os-network` (or the local
  project service in single-tenant mode) before accepting a
  `project_id`, returning 404 on unknown projects.
- Gate the direct CDP spawn path on aura-os-server behind the new
  `browser-cdp` feature and enable it in the desktop app, so the
  server crate can be consumed without chromiumoxide when not needed.
- Reject `Navigate` client messages with invalid URLs at the manager
  edge via `validate_url`, so authz + URL hygiene happen before the
  backend adapter ever sees them.
- Add `apps/aura-os-server/tests/browser_api_tests.rs` covering the
  ownership filter on list, the 404 on cross-user kill, and the
  project-not-found path on spawn.

No user-visible API shape change; responses are now just correctly
scoped to the caller.

rawCommits.subject: agent_bootstrap: add POST /api/super-agent/cleanup for one-shot CEO dedupe
rawCommits.body:
The dedupe logic added in f62eb416 only runs as a side effect of `setup_ceo_agent`, which is fine for steady state but awkward when you're staring at a list of 14 stray CEOs and want them gone now. Pull the sweep out into `dedupe_ceo_agents()` and hang a dedicated handler off it that does only the delete pass — never creates a new CEO — so the user can trigger cleanup explicitly without any side effects.

- `dedupe_ceo_agents` finds CEO records via the same `looks_like_ceo` probe as setup, sorts them oldest-first (via `sort_ceo_candidates_oldest_first`), keeps the oldest, and best-effort `network.delete_agent` the rest, returning a `DedupeOutcome` with the deleted / failed id lists so the new handler can surface them.

- `setup_ceo_agent` now just calls `dedupe_ceo_agents` and branches on the canonical result instead of inlining the sweep.

- `cleanup_ceo_agents` is the new handler wired to `POST /api/super-agent/cleanup`. Response is `{ kept?, deleted[], failed[] }` so the caller can confirm which duplicate rows went away. No create side effect: an account with zero CEOs gets `{ deleted: [], failed: [] }` and can then call `/api/super-agent/setup` to bootstrap one.

- Add `superAgentApi.cleanup()` on the frontend for UI callers.

rawCommits.body:
My earlier `isSuperAgent` fallback made the TS check match every duplicate CEO by name+role, which incidentally broke the `fetchAgents` auto-heal: `hasSuperAgent` was always true, so `api.superAgent.setup()` never ran, so the backend's in-setup dedupe never triggered — leaving the user staring at 14 CEOs forever.

Now `fetchAgents` counts matches instead of testing for any:

- `=== 0` super-agents -> call `setup()` to bootstrap one (unchanged).

- `> 1` super-agents -> call the new `cleanup()` endpoint and refetch if it deleted anything, so the list collapses to the single canonical record the server kept. Cleanup failures are silently swallowed — duplicates will just stick around until the next refresh.

- Exactly one super-agent is the happy path and does nothing extra.

rawCommits.subject: LoginView: suppress chrome while auth is still restoring
rawCommits.body:
The auth store starts with `{ user: null, isLoading: true }` whenever the
sync localStorage mirror is empty at module import (post-upgrade, PWA
eviction, cross-tab writes while the session still lives in IndexedDB).
`RequireAuth` already treats that unresolved state as "don't paint yet"
and shows a spinner, so protected routes honor the shell-first boot.
`LoginView` only guarded against already-authenticated users, so if the
initial URL was `/login` the form flashed until `restoreSession()`
hydrated from IndexedDB.

Extend the existing guard to also return null while `isLoading`, matching
`RequireAuth`'s gate. Once `restoreSession` settles, the form renders
normally for truly-unauthenticated users and `useLoginForm`'s redirect
effect navigates away for authenticated ones.

Adds a focused LoginView render test covering all three states:
loading, authenticated, and unauthenticated-after-restore.

rawCommits.body:
The CEO super-agent is universe-scoped and wasn't bound to any project,
so `setup_agent_chat_persistence` failed for it and the SSE stream
prepended a "Chat history could not be saved - storage is unavailable"
banner on every direct CEO chat.

Fix it on both ends:
- /api/super-agent/setup now also ensures the canonical CEO has a
  project-agent binding somewhere. If none of the user's existing
  projects already references it, the handler finds-or-creates a
  dedicated "Home" project in the CEO's org (marked with a stable
  sentinel so a user-authored project literally called "Home" is never
  adopted) and binds the CEO there. Best-effort: network/storage errors
  are logged and swallowed so transient failures don't break setup.
- fetchAgents always calls setup() once per app session (not just when
  superAgents.length === 0). setup() is idempotent on both the bootstrap
  and the ensure-home paths, so this is what heals existing accounts
  whose canonical CEO had its binding orphaned by the recent dedupe
  sweep. The flag is reset on sign-out so account switches re-run it.

rawCommits.subject: desktop-shell: handle lazy provider remount when retargeting sidekick width
rawCommits.body:
The previous retarget effect read `mainPanelRef.current` synchronously and
only retried across a few animation frames. When switching from an app
without a Provider (e.g. Projects) to one with a lazy-wrapped Provider
(e.g. Tasks), the `ActiveProvider` swap unmounts the main panel div while
the lazy chunk loads, so the effect runs with `mainPanelRef.current` null
and its rAF retries can easily exhaust before the new div mounts. The
ResizeObserver fallback then bailed because it checked the same null ref.
Second-time navigation worked only because the lazy chunk was cached and
the div stayed mounted.

Back the main panel with callback-ref-driven state so the retarget effect
re-runs when the div mounts or unmounts. Track the currently-applied app
id (rather than the previous one) and only mark an app as applied after a
successful `setSize`, so prerequisite-missing runs retry transparently on
the next dep change. Keep a ResizeObserver for the case where the new div
mounts at width 0 while the provider is still suspending.

rawCommits.subject: chat: dedupe harness installed_tools by name before sending to LLM
rawCommits.body:
The LLM API rejects the whole request with a 400
"tools: Tool names must be unique." the instant the same tool name
appears twice in SessionConfig.installed_tools. After the CEO
permissions safety net started emitting the full cross-agent tool
manifest, any overlap between workspace/trusted-MCP tools and the
cross-agent manifest (e.g. a local trusted MCP that re-exports
list_agents or send_to_agent) started crashing the whole turn.

Route both chat entry points (agent chat + instance chat) through a
single build_session_installed_tools helper that dedupes by tool name
with first-occurrence-wins ordering, so workspace / integration tools
(org-scoped endpoints + auth) take precedence over any identically
named cross-agent copy. Log a warn! with the dropped names so we can
still track the overlap down from logs instead of masking it silently.

rawCommits.subject: agents: stamp JWT + org id on cross-agent tools before shipping to harness
rawCommits.body:
`build_cross_agent_tools` emits every `InstalledTool` with
`ToolAuth::None`, so the harness POSTs to `/api/agent_tools/:name`
with no `Authorization` header and the dispatcher's `AuthJwt`
extractor returns `401 {"error":"missing authorization"}`. This
surfaced immediately after the previous URL-absolutisation fix —
the harness could now reach the endpoint but couldn't authenticate.

Add `stamp_agent_tool_auth` in aura-os-agent-runtime that rewrites
`ToolAuth::None` → `ToolAuth::Headers { Authorization: Bearer <jwt>,
x-aura-org-id: <org> }` for every cross-agent dispatcher entry, and
call it in `build_session_installed_tools` right after
`build_cross_agent_tools` (before absolutisation so the matching
path check stays simple). Workspace / MCP integration tools keep
their own bearer tokens because the helper scopes its rewrite to
endpoints under `AGENT_TOOL_PATH_PREFIX`.

Regression tests pin the shape of the forwarded headers, the
personal-account (no org) fallback, whitespace org-id handling,
empty-JWT no-op, and that the helper also recognises the absolute
URL form in case the call order ever gets reshuffled.

rawCommits.subject: agents: stop stripping send_to_agent and realign it with harness contract
rawCommits.body:
The CEO in the Agents app reported it had no messaging tool when asked
to send a message to another org-level agent. Two bugs cooperated:

1. `HARNESS_NATIVE_TOOL_NAMES` listed the five cross-agent names
   (`spawn_agent`, `send_to_agent`, `agent_lifecycle`, `get_agent_state`,
   `delegate_task`) on the premise that the harness sidecar advertises
   them via `visible_tools(ToolProfile::Agent, _)` and would otherwise
   produce an Anthropic "Tool names must be unique" 400. Walking
   `aura-harness/crates/aura-tools/src/catalog.rs` shows those entries
   carry non-empty `required_capabilities` and that
   `populate_tool_definitions` (aura-node/src/session/ws_handler.rs)
   calls `visible_tools` with `None` for permissions, so the harness
   never emits them. The strip therefore deleted the ONLY copy the LLM
   would see, which is why the CEO apologised instead of invoking the
   tool. Drop those five names from the strip list and flip the
   corresponding test to assert they survive; add a focused regression
   test for the CEO case.

2. Even un-stripped, the server-side `SendToAgentTool` required
   `project_id` + `agent_instance_id` + `message` and POSTed to the
   project-scoped chat endpoint, which doesn't fit the Agents app
   scenario (CEO addressing Machina by org-level `agent_id`). Realign
   with the harness-native contract
   (`aura-harness/crates/aura-tools/src/agents/send_to_agent.rs`): take
   `agent_id` + `content` (+ optional `attachments`) and POST to
   `/api/agents/:agent_id/events/stream`, which is already served by
   `send_agent_event_stream`. Refresh the `ceo.rs` schema assertion to
   match.

rawCommits.body:
Every CEO-preset turn was paying for redundant work before the harness
could even see the user's message. The "who are my agents" case is
the cleanest example — it routed through ~55 InstalledTools, two
`list_orgs`/`list_projects`/`list_project_agents` fan-outs, N
per-session event probes, and a full `ToolRegistry` rebuild on every
single tool dispatch. Restructure so that hot path does the minimum
amount of work:

- Cache `ToolRegistry::with_all_tools()` and `tool_metadata_map()` in
  process-wide `LazyLock`s. The ~55 `Arc<dyn AgentTool>` entries are
  stateless, so the registry is safe to share; the dispatcher and the
  CEO installed-tools builder now reuse the same instance every turn.
- Dedupe `find_matching_project_agents` in `send_agent_event_stream`
  and run persistence setup + history loading concurrently with
  `tokio::join!` instead of serially.
- Parallelize `list_all_projects_from_network` with `join_all` across
  orgs.
- Fetch `integrations_for_org_with_token` once per turn and feed both
  the installed-tools catalog and the installed-integrations list from
  the same slice via the new `*_with_integrations` variants.
- In `resolve_chat_session`, pick the newest session by sort key and
  drop the per-session `list_events(limit=1)` liveness probe.
- Add a 5s TTL cache around `list_agents` / `get_agent` network
  lookups, invalidated by mutating agent tools
  (`assign_agent_to_project`, `create_agent`, `update_agent`,
  `delete_agent`).

rawCommits.subject: agents: fix spurious 404 when CEO messages another agent
rawCommits.body:
send_agent_event_stream resolved the target agent through
AgentService::get_agent_async, which silently ignored the request
JWT and instead read from SettingsStore::get_jwt() - a shared
in-memory cache that raced under concurrent requests (e.g. the UI
polling remote_agent/state for every agent while the CEO issues
send_to_agent), causing aura-network to be queried with the wrong
bearer and returning 404. The local-shadow fallback also never fired
because list_agents/get_agent tools never hydrated it.

Thread the caller's JWT explicitly via a new get_agent_with_jwt,
only fall back to the local shadow on a true NotFound, and surface
other upstream errors as 5xx instead of masking them as "agent not
found". Hydrate the shadow from list_agents/get_agent so the CEO's
own catalog lookups seed the fallback path.

rawCommits.body:
The bottom-left context utilization indicator in the chat input bar was
only populated after the first AssistantMessageEnd event of a new stream,
so entering an agent chat with prior history always showed a blank until
the user sent another message.

Add a lightweight GET /context-usage endpoint (standalone + instance
variants) that returns the latest session's context_usage_estimate, and
seed useContextUsageStore on chat mount via a new
useHydrateContextUtilization hook. The hook guards against clobbering a
live stream turn, a value already in the store, and a zero response.

To keep the "new session" button honest across remounts and reset-API
failures, add a per-streamKey reset sentinel to the store:
markResetPending is called by handleNewSession, isResetPending blocks
hydration, and setContextUtilization auto-clears the sentinel on the
next assistant turn so the indicator re-enables naturally.

Session.context_usage_estimate, but chat sessions never write that

session's events newest-first, finds the most recent

rawCommits.subject: agents: route send_to_agent through the local server, not aura-network
rawCommits.body:
The `send_to_agent` tool posted to `{network.base_url()}/api/agents/
{id}/events/stream`, but that endpoint is owned by aura-os-server —
aura-network doesn't expose per-agent event streams. With
AURA_NETWORK_URL pointing at the cloud service (the default), every
CEO-to-agent message 404'd before reaching the harness. The previous
fix to `send_agent_event_stream`'s resolver was necessary but
insufficient because the request was hitting the wrong host entirely.

Prefer `ctx.local_server_base_url` (always populated from
AURA_SERVER_BASE_URL or the bound host+port in app_builder) and
reuse the shared `local_http_client`. Fall back to the network
client only if the local URL is absent, so test harnesses that
stand up a mock network keep working. The channel send in
`open_harness_chat_stream` delivers the user turn synchronously
before the SSE stream is returned, so dropping the response body
after checking status still schedules the target agent's turn.

rawCommits.subject: chat: stop stale identical-content history row swallowing fresh optimistic bubble
rawCommits.body:
Standalone CEO chat (and any other view that renders through
`useConversationSnapshot` with `hydrateToStream: false`) was silently
dropping the user's own just-sent message when an earlier turn in
history had the same content. Typing "test" a second time made the
optimistic bubble invisible even though the LLM still streamed a reply.

`combineStoredAndStreamMessages` scanned stored history backward for
*any* role+content match and dedup'd the optimistic row against it.
With a prior identical "test" already persisted, the fresh `temp-*`
bubble got matched to the older row and vanished.

Rework the merge so an optimistic stream row is only treated as
"already persisted" when it is positionally anchored to the current
turn — either the tail of `stored` sequence-matches the full stream
(history has fully caught up) or the candidate stored index sits
adjacent to an index already matched by a stable `event_id` id match,
or is the final stored row. Unanchored content matches (i.e. older
duplicates) no longer suppress the live bubble.

Updated the existing dedup test to reflect the real production shape
(stream holds both the optimistic user row and the `handleEventSaved`-
substituted saved-assistant anchor) and added regressions for the
repeated-short-message case and the full turn-tail sequence match.

rawCommits.subject: fix(agent-chat): surface persistence status to send_to_agent so silent drops become visible
rawCommits.body:
The CEO's send_to_agent tool was reporting "Message sent!" even when
the target agent's turn never reached storage, so messages appeared
to deliver but never showed up in the recipient's chat tab.

`send_agent_event_stream` silently short-circuits persistence when
`find_matching_project_agents` returns empty (agent exists in the
network catalog but isn't bound to any project in storage). The SSE
stream still runs, the harness still processes the message, but
nothing is written to the session event log — and the UI's
`/api/agents/:id/events` reader only reads from storage, so the
message is invisible.

Pipe the persistence outcome back through response headers so fire-
and-forget callers that only read the response head can detect this:

- `x-aura-chat-persisted: true|false`
- `x-aura-chat-session-id: <storage session id>`
- `x-aura-chat-project-id: <project id used for persistence>`

`SendToAgentTool` now reads these headers and returns
`is_error: true` with an actionable warning when persistence was
skipped, so the CEO stops reporting false success and the user gets
told to bind the target agent to a project first.

rawCommits.subject: agents: batch list_agents shadow writes so settings.json is rewritten at most once per request
rawCommits.body:
GET /api/agents called save_agent_shadow per row inside the hot path, and
each call ended in SettingsStore::persist_cf, which re-serializes and
fs::writes the entire settings.json under a held RwLock write guard on the
tokio runtime thread. Listing N agents meant N full rewrites of a file that
also holds projects/orgs/integrations/runtime configs, so response times
scaled linearly with both agent count and settings size.

Add AgentService::save_agent_shadows_if_changed that serializes each agent,
diffs against the currently stored bytes, and submits only the changed rows
as a single write_batch (one persist_cf regardless of N). The list_agents
handler now builds the response purely from in-memory reads and fires the
batched shadow flush on a spawn_blocking task so disk I/O never gates the
response. repair_agent_name_in_place is split into a mutate-only variant
(repair_agent_name_only) used by the list path, keeping the original
persist-on-change semantics for single-agent callers. hydrate_shadow_from_network
(used by the LLM list_agents / get_agent tools) is rewritten on top of the
same primitive.

Covered by new tests: unchanged inputs leave settings.json mtime untouched,
a single mutated row produces exactly one batched write, and empty input
is a no-op.

rawCommits.subject: fix(context): stop CEO chats from filling context in two turns
rawCommits.body:
Two compounding sources pushed the CEO's prompt to 100% utilization on
simple two-message chats:

1. `list_agents` / `get_agent` returned the full `NetworkAgent` record,
   including multi-KB `system_prompt` and `personality` strings per
   agent. A single lookup to resolve a name before `send_to_agent`
   dumped tens of KB into the tool_result -- which then rode along
   with every subsequent turn because the harness's `Session.messages`
   is append-only.

2. `render_conversation_text` embedded that entire JSON verbatim into
   the flat assistant-text cold-start replay, so restarting the chat
   re-seeded the same bloat.

Changes:

- `ListAgentsTool` / `GetAgentTool` now return a compact summary
  (id/name/role/icon/tags/skills/listing_status). `get_agent` exposes
  an opt-in `include_details=true` to fetch the full record on demand.
- `render_conversation_text` caps each tool_use input / tool_result
  content blob at `TOOL_BLOB_MAX_BYTES` (2KB) with a
  "[truncated N bytes]" marker. Tool blobs in turns outside the
  recent window (`HISTORY_RECENT_TURNS`=2) are capped even tighter
  (256B) so long histories leave only a breadcrumb.
- `send_agent_event_stream` logs the flat-text `conversation_messages`
  byte size and warns above 64KB so future regressions surface in
  logs without needing a user bug report.

Alongside this, pick up the in-flight CEO manifest work: replace the
per-turn intent-classifier narrowing with a static `CEO_CORE_TOOLS`
allowlist (the classifier was silently dropping `send_to_agent` on
trivial prompts and shipping ~55 tool definitions per turn on others).

Tests: 5 new unit tests in `chat.rs` exercising `truncate_for_history`
(noop / truncation / char-boundary) and the window-aware cap in
`session_events_to_conversation_history`; 1 new unit test in
`agent_tools.rs` asserting `to_agent_summary` drops the verbose
fields; CEO manifest guardrail tests updated for the static allowlist.

`list_agents` turn because:

rawCommits.body:
Sidebar, ProjectAgentSetup, and the CEO's `list_agents` tool were
filtering by `user_id` alone, so teammates' agents never showed up
even when the caller had the org membership to see them. Pipe the
active `org_id` end-to-end:

- `GET /api/agents` accepts an `org_id` query param; when set, the
  handler calls aura-network's `list_agents_by_org`, which drops the

- `api.agents.list(orgId?)` forwards the param from the UI.
- `useAgentStore.fetchAgents` and `ProjectAgentSetupView` pass the
  active org id from `useOrgStore`, falling back to the unscoped list
  during the brief first-mount window before orgs settle.

  against aura-network when no `X-Aura-Org-Id` header is present,
  so sessions bootstrapped without an agent org_id (fresh-install
  CEO, direct curl, older harness) still get a real org. When
  resolution genuinely fails it returns the `"default"` sentinel.
- `ListAgentsTool` recognizes the sentinel and falls back to the
  user-scoped call instead of sending `?org_id=default` to

rawCommits.body:
CEOs asked "what's today's date" were falling back to
`run_command date`, which on Windows hits cmd.exe's interactive
`date` built-in (no `/t`), exits with code 1, and dumps garbage to
the tool_result. PowerShell's `date` is an alias for `Get-Date`,
POSIX `date` takes different format flags — every shell answers
differently, or not at all.

Add `GetCurrentTimeTool` (Tier 1, `System` domain) that returns
`iso_local`, `iso_utc`, a human-readable string, and `unix_ms`
straight from `chrono`, without spawning a shell. Register it in
the tool registry and add it to `CEO_CORE_TOOLS` so the CEO picks
it up without loading a domain.

rawCommits.subject: fix(send_to_agent): drain SSE and return target's reply to caller
rawCommits.body:
Previously the tool stopped reading at the HTTP response headers and
threw away the SSE body, so when the CEO asked Barret a question it
got back `{sent: true, persisted: true}` and nothing to reason about.
That's the "Agent was not able to send a response back" bug.

Changes:
- Parse the SSE body after a 2xx, concatenating `text_delta.text`
  payloads until we see `assistant_message_end`.
- Apply three budgets so a runaway target can't balloon the caller's
  tool_result (which rides in the harness conversation history for
  the rest of the session — the same mechanism that drove the CEO's
  context to 100% with `list_agents`):
    * DRAIN_MAX_WAIT        = 60s
    * DRAIN_MAX_BYTES       = 256 KiB of socket traffic
    * DRAIN_MAX_REPLY_CHARS = 32 KiB of reply text
  Codepoint-safe truncation at the char boundary.
- Ignore every non-`text_delta` event type (`tool_use_start`,
  `tool_result`, etc.) — those belong in the target agent's own
  session, not in the caller's tool_result.
- Return `reply`, `reply_complete`, `reply_timed_out`, and
  `reply_truncated` so the calling LLM can narrate gracefully when
  the drain hit a budget.

Added two unit tests exercising a real SSE byte stream: one end-to-
end happy path (two text_deltas + assistant_message_end → concatenated
reply, `reply_complete: true`), and one that pins the "ignore
non-text events" invariant.

rawCommits.subject: fix(skills): keep user-created marker after harness overwrites SKILL.md
rawCommits.body:
User-created skills were landing under "Available" instead of
"My Skills" because the harness's `POST /api/skills` handler writes
its OWN SKILL.md to `~/.aura/skills/<name>/` (different frontmatter
shape — `name:` included, `user-invocable:` hyphenated, no
`source:` marker). Our `create_skill` was writing first and then
the harness was silently clobbering the file, so `list_my_skills`
never found the marker and the skill fell through to the catalog
listing.

Reorder `create_skill`: call the harness to register first (let it
write whatever), then write our marker-bearing frontmatter last so
the last-writer-wins race lands on our file.

The existing regression test used a recording mock that didn't
write any files, so it missed the clobber. Add a `start_clobbering_
mock_harness` that models the real harness's write-on-POST behaviour
and a new test `create_skill_marker_survives_harness_overwrite`
that fails on the old ordering and passes on the new one. Both are
`#[cfg(unix)]`-gated like the other HOME-overriding tests.

rawCommits.body:
Deleting a user-authored skill was using `window.confirm` +
`window.alert`, which look out of place inside the app chrome
("127.0.0.1:5173 says ..."). Mirror the agent-delete flow
(`DeleteConfirmModal` in AgentInfoPanel) with a local
`DeleteSkillConfirmModal` backed by the existing zui Modal:

- A danger-variant Confirm button with an in-button spinner while
  the delete is in flight.
- The cancel button is disabled mid-delete, and `onClose` is a
  no-op while deleting so the modal can't be dismissed into a
  half-state.
- Errors render inline in the modal via `deleteError`, replacing
  the browser `alert` popup.

Also splits the previous single handler into `requestDeleteMySkill`
(opens the modal) and `confirmDeleteMySkill` (performs the work
when the user clicks Delete), so the button click is decoupled
from the async work.

rawCommits.subject: ui(skills): silent refresh + optimistic remove on skill delete
rawCommits.body:
Deleting a skill was flashing the whole Skills sidekick section —
Installed, My Skills, and Available all briefly disappeared behind
the loading guard while `fetchData` ran. Every mutation
(install/uninstall/delete/create) did the same thing because
`fetchData` unconditionally toggled `loading=true`.

Make post-mutation refreshes silent and update local state
optimistically so the UI changes in place:

- `fetchData({ silent })` skips the `loading` toggle when true, so
  the three collapsible sections stay mounted with their existing
  rows while the refetch happens in the background. Initial load
  still flashes loading (no existing data to preserve).
- `confirmDeleteMySkill` optimistically removes the deleted skill
  from `mySkills`, `installations`, and `catalog` before kicking
  off the silent reconciliation fetch — the row vanishes instantly
  rather than waiting for the round-trip.
- `handleInstall`, `handleUninstall`, `CreateSkillModal.onCreated`,
  and `SkillShopModal.onInstalled` now all pass `{ silent: true }`
  so single-skill actions never trigger a full section refresh.

rawCommits.body:
Tier D of the decoupling review: extract every concrete `AgentTool` impl
and the CEO-template wiring into a new peer crate `aura-os-agent-tools`
so `aura-os-agent-runtime` no longer pulls the full domain graph
(projects, tasks, sessions, orgs, etc.). Runtime keeps the `AgentTool`
trait, `ToolRegistry`, `AgentToolContext`, `AgentRuntimeService`,
`events`, and `state`.

Also lands the security + diagnostics plumbing the cross-agent
dispatcher needs:
- `policy` module: per-call capability re-check (`check_capabilities`)
  plus a TTL-bounded `PermissionsCache` keyed by stamped id so
  local-only installs stop 403'ing on `list_agents` /
  `get_fleet_status`.
- `audit` module: in-memory ring buffer + `tracing` target
  `agent_tool_audit` for every invocation, with
  `AURA_TOOL_POLICY_MODE=audit` safety valve that records `deny_audit`
  instead of failing.
- Regression test at `apps/aura-os-server/tests/agent_tools_policy_test.rs`.

Workspace hygiene: hoist shared `[package]` fields, `[lints]`, and
common dependency versions into the root `Cargo.toml` so every crate
inherits via `workspace = true`.

rawCommits.subject: fix(sidekick): persist completed task turns and fix malformed WRITE row
rawCommits.body:
The Sidekick run panel was dropping tasks as soon as they finished and
rendering WRITE rows with an ellipsis filename and an empty code area
with just a "1" line number while a run was still streaming. Both issues
came from the same stream finalization path.

- finalizeStream now consolidates the buffered turn (text + unsnapshotted
  tool calls + timeline) on terminal outcomes even when closureIsStreaming
  is true, so TaskCompleted/TaskFailed no longer discards the last turn.
  Already-snapshotted tool calls are filtered out to prevent duplicates
  across a handleAssistantTurnBoundary followed by a finalizeStream.
- handleToolCallStarted no longer seeds empty path/content for write_file
  and edit_file, so FileBlock can render its compact "Writing code..."
  header instead of a blank code surface.
- FileBlock falls back to the tool's phase label when the path has not
  arrived yet and only force-expands when real content exists.
- use-task-stream hardens tool id handling: ToolUseStart now generates a
  UUID when the id is missing or blank, and ToolCallSnapshot drops
  id-less snapshots that would otherwise create an orphan pending tool
  call and leave the streaming banner stuck after the run ended.
- CompletedTaskOutput shows a muted placeholder when neither stream
  events nor hydrated task output exist, so a completed run is never a
  bare header.

Adds unit tests for the new finalize behavior, the dropped empty seed,
and the CompletedTaskOutput placeholder.

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): restore NULL-org agents in CEO `list_agents` tool
rawCommits.body:
The server-side `/api/agents?org_id=X` handler was patched in commit
23ad8d56 to run the org-scoped and user-scoped lookups concurrently
and merge by `agent_id`, so legacy rows with `org_id IS NULL` don't
disappear from the sidebar after commit 8a085cc0 made listing
strictly org-scoped. The CEO's `list_agents` LLM tool was never
given the same treatment and still issued a single
`list_agents_by_org` call, so the CEO saw only 4 of the 15 agents
visible in the sidebar whenever the caller owned NULL-org records.

Mirror the server handler's strategy inside `ListAgentsTool::execute`:
fire both calls via `tokio::join!`, merge deduplicating by id with
the org record winning on conflict, and fall back to org-only with
a warn log if the user-scoped backstop errors. Factored the merge
into a pure helper `merge_network_agents` with unit tests covering
id collisions, user-only inclusion, order stability, and the empty
backstop fallback.

rawCommits.subject: fix(integrations): align control-plane base URL with AURA_SERVER_BASE_URL
rawCommits.body:
`control_plane_api_base_url()` was reading `AURA_CONTROL_PLANE_API_BASE_URL`
(a name set nowhere in the repo and undocumented), so on production it
always fell through to `http://127.0.0.1:<port>` with `0.0.0.0`
force-normalized to loopback. That URL was then stamped into every
cross-agent tool endpoint via `absolutize_agent_tool_endpoints`, so
remote harnesses (aura-swarm on Render) hit "external tool callback
unreachable: http://127.0.0.1:3100/api/agent_tools/send_to_agent" on
the first tool call.

The server already resolves its own public URL from `AURA_SERVER_BASE_URL`
in `app_builder.rs` and hands it to the agent runtime for `send_to_agent`.
Switch the helper to read the same env var so both self-callback paths
share one source of truth, and document the variable in `.env.example`
and `docs/render-deployment.md` (plus a troubleshooting note for the
exact error string).

rawCommits.subject: fix(auth): stop login panel flashing at startup for authenticated users
rawCommits.body:
Commit e01c5745 replaced the `hasResolvedInitialSession` gate added in
9da41eb4 with `isLoading`, arguing they were equivalent. They are not:
`isLoading` starts `false` whenever `getInitialAuthState()` finds a
cached session, so `LoginView`'s guard (`isAuthenticated || isLoading`)
permits the form to paint for one frame on cold start whenever the URL
happens to be `/login` (persisted URL, browser reload, or a brief 401
during boot-time revalidation) before `useLoginForm`'s redirect commits.

Reintroduce `hasResolvedInitialSession` on the auth store, flipped to
`true` in `restoreSession`'s `finally` and inside `login` / `register` /
`logout` setters. Gate only `LoginView` on it; leave `RequireAuth` keyed
off `isLoading` so authenticated users still render their shell on the
very first paint (preserving the perceived-load-time win from e01c5745).

Also delay `signalDesktopReady()` from the login route until the first
restore resolves, so the desktop window never appears on a blank login
route while the redirect is still pending.

Tests: add pre-resolution cached-session /login case to LoginView and
hasResolvedInitialSession assertions across auth-store actions.

rawCommits.subject: fix(auth): redirect /login to / at route level when a session is cached
rawCommits.body:
Follow-up to 5de2b45a. The prior fix guarded `LoginView`'s render with
`hasResolvedInitialSession`, but `LoginView` still mounted on startup at
`/login` for authenticated users before `useLoginForm`'s redirect effect
committed. In the desktop production bundle that single frame was
visible as a brief login panel.

Add a `LoginRoute` wrapper in the router that calls `useAuth()` and
short-circuits to `<Navigate to="/" replace />` whenever `isAuthenticated`
is true. `getInitialAuthState()` already seeds the store synchronously
from the localStorage session mirror, so returning users never mount
`LoginView` at all — the redirect happens on the first React render.

Keep the in-component `LoginView` gate in place as defense-in-depth for
transitions like a boot-time 401 clearing the cached user.

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): auto-bind new agents to a Home project so first chat persists
rawCommits.body:
New agents created via `POST /api/agents` had no `project_agent` binding,
so the first chat turn hard-failed with `Chat persistence unavailable:
target agent is not bound to any project in storage...`. Only the CEO
preset avoided this because `setup_ceo_agent` runs a dedicated
`ensure_ceo_home_project_and_binding` helper; nothing equivalent ran for
regular user-created agents.

Generalize that helper into a shared `home_project` module and call it
from two places:

- `crud::create_agent` after the final `save_agent_shadow`, so a freshly
  created agent is immediately chat-ready (best-effort; failures logged
  and swallowed like the CEO path).
- `chat::setup_agent_chat_persistence` as a lazy repair when no matching
  `project_agent` row is found. This self-heals existing orphan agents
  on their next chat attempt without any manual intervention or admin
  endpoint, then retries `find_matching_project_agents` once.

The helper recognizes both the new `[aura:agent-home]` marker and the
legacy `[aura:ceo-home]` marker during the find step, so existing prod
Home projects are reused rather than duplicated. Both CEO bootstrap call
sites now route through the generalized helper.

rawCommits.subject: fix(auth): hold the whole route tree until auth is resolved at startup
rawCommits.body:
Previous attempts to stop the login panel from flashing at startup
gated progressively deeper layers: first `LoginView`'s render
(9da41eb4), then `/login`'s route element (d18b401d). The user still
sees a sub-300ms login-form flash before the shell across desktop
prod, desktop dev, and browser, which means some React path is still
mounting `LoginView` on an intermediate render that none of those
guards catch.

Rethink the fix from the ground up: don't mount any routes at all
until the boot-time `restoreSession()` has finished. `App` now
subscribes to `hasResolvedInitialSession` and returns `null` while it
is `false`, which — combined with the existing `background:#000` on
`<body>` — produces exactly the "black screen then final view"
sequence the user expects. The `useEffect` that drives
`hydrateStoredAuth` → `bootstrapNativeTestAuth` → `restoreSession`
still fires on mount (React runs effects even when a component
renders `null`), so the flag flips on the normal timeline.

Also simplify `LoginView`: with the App-level gate in place its
`hasResolvedInitialSession` read and guard are redundant, so drop
them. Keep the `isAuthenticated || isLoading` short-circuit — it
still covers post-login in-flight states and manual
`refreshSession()`s.

Trade-off: authenticated users pay roughly one extra
`restoreSession()` round-trip of blank screen before the shell
paints. On desktop this is invisible because the Tauri window stays
hidden until `signalDesktopReady()` fires from `AppShell`'s mount
(which now happens one round-trip later). In the browser, users see
a short black screen instead of a flash, which matches the expected
startup appearance.

Tests: drop the pre-resolution LoginView cases (the guarantee now
lives in `App`, not `LoginView`) and remove `hasResolvedInitialSession`
from the LoginView test stub.

Made-with: Cursor

rendered.title: One Agent model, a new in-app browser, and a marketplace debut
rendered.intro: A heavy day across the stack: the long-running super-agent unification finally collapses into a single permissions-driven Agent model, a real Chromium-backed browser lands inside the projects sidekick, the Talent Marketplace ships its first end-to-end slice, and a long tail of CEO chat reliability work makes cross-agent messaging actually work end to end. Mobile parity, login-flash fixes, and release infrastructure hardening round out the day.

entries.title: Aura platform overhaul: unified agents, in-app browser, marketplace, and a long CEO reliability arc
entries.summary: The bulk of today's work: the super-agent path is collapsed into a permissions-driven Agent model, a real headless-browser surface lands in the projects sidekick, the Talent Marketplace ships its first slice, and a long sequence of fixes finally makes the CEO's cross-agent chat reliable end to end.

items.text: Collapsed the dedicated super-agent stack into a single Agent model: an agent's powers now come purely from a required AgentPermissions bundle (CEO is just the ceo_preset), the legacy in-process SuperAgentStream and host-mode toggle are gone, and the crates were renamed from aura-os-super-agent{,-profile} to aura-os-agent-{runtime,templates}. The interface follows suit with a permissions-derived super-agent check and a new Permissions sidekick tab for editing scope and capabilities.

Shipped a real in-app browser in the Projects sidekick: a new aura-os-browser crate plus REST/WebSocket endpoints back a Chromium CDP backend with screencast frames, real input forwarding, project-aware initial URLs from terminal-discovered localhost ports, multi-tab UI, and per-user session ownership with project-scoped authorization. Address bar, wheel, and tab UX got polish and tests, and Chrome/Edge are now auto-discovered with isolated temp profiles.

Launched the first end-to-end slice of the Agent Talent Marketplace: a new top-level app with sidebar, sort/filter, and hire flow, plus first-class listing_status and expertise fields on Agent records, a backfill script, and a migration doc. The Marketplace sidebar uses the shared OverlayScrollbar to match other apps.

Reorganized the taskbar and integrations surface: pinned apps and the new LayoutGrid Apps organizer let users reorder and hide apps with drag-and-drop, integrations moved out of Team Settings into a dedicated Integrations app grouped by category, AI providers were split into their own Providers group (gated behind a feature flag), and the bottom taskbar got a true-center layout.

Made CEO cross-agent chat actually work: cross-agent tool endpoints are now absolutized and stamped with JWT + org headers, harness-native and dispatcher tools are deduped before reaching Anthropic, send_to_agent posts to the local server with a real reply drained from the SSE stream, persistence failures surface as hard errors instead of silent drops, and a new Home project is auto-bound for direct CEO chats. Agent listings are scoped to the active org while preserving NULL-org records, and a corrupted CEO permissions bundle is repaired on read.

Slashed CEO chat context bloat and latency: list_agents and get_agent now return slim id/name/role summaries scoped to the active org, tool blobs in conversation history are truncated, the CEO turn caches its tool registry and runs persistence + history loads concurrently with parallel project lookups, and a hover popover on the chat context indicator now shows used vs total tokens. A new cross-platform get_current_time tool replaces shell-dependent date lookups.

Built out the Skills experience: users can author, list, and delete their own skills via a new "My Skills" section in the agent Skills tab, with create now registering with the harness so new skills appear immediately, an in-app delete confirmation modal replacing browser dialogs, optimistic and silent reconciliation on mutations, and frontmatter ordering that survives the harness's own SKILL.md rewrite.

Added an installed-tools diagnostic and security plumbing for cross-agent tools: a new Permissions panel surfaces the exact tool list shipped to the harness with missing-registration warnings, capability-gated readProject/writeProject grants now light up native project tools automatically, and a fresh aura-os-agent-tools split adds a per-call policy re-check, a permissions cache, and an audit ring buffer with an AURA_TOOL_POLICY_MODE=audit safety valve.

Reliability and polish across the desktop shell: fixed a Windows auto-update sidecar lock that was blocking installs, hardened the update banner against being painted over, made the desktop window wait for a real React-mounted UI before showing, gave each app its own remembered sidekick width with lazy-provider-safe retargeting, repaired blank agent names and missing chat-history previews, and added an Agent right-click Edit action plus a simplified agent editor with a System Prompt field.

Project workspace and storage cleanups: a per-machine Local workspace folder override on Projects and Agents (with a server-resolved default path surfaced inline in the picker), a renamed on-disk store/ directory with first-launch migration from the old db/ path, and a split Github / Orbit settings layout with a read-only Orbit URL.

Auth startup hardening: cached sessions are now seeded synchronously from localStorage so the API client has a token on the very first call, login routes are gated until the initial restoreSession resolves, and the login panel no longer flashes for already-authenticated users on cold start.

Release infrastructure: changelog generation moved to Claude Opus 4.7 in its own publishing workflow, with version derived from release artifacts, immutable nightly release URLs in updater manifests, and a hardened metadata handoff between release and changelog jobs.

Deployment correctness for remote harnesses: the integrations control-plane base URL now reads AURA_SERVER_BASE_URL like the rest of the server (documented in .env.example and the Render deployment guide), so cross-agent tool callbacks no longer point at 127.0.0.1 from production swarm nodes.

entries.title: Changelog generation: drop deprecated Anthropic temperature param
entries.summary: Maintenance fix to the daily changelog generator after switching models.

items.text: Removed the deprecated temperature parameter from the daily changelog generation script so calls to Anthropic stop emitting an unsupported argument.

entries.title: Mobile parity, regression coverage, and a router-level login redirect
entries.summary: Mobile remote flows reach parity with desktop and gain regression coverage, alongside a router-level guard that eliminates the last login-form flash for authenticated users.

items.text: Brought mobile to parity with the remote app: rebuilt MobileTopbar, MobileShell, MobileOrganizationView, and the Projects routes (Files, Tasks, Work, Agent setup) so navigation, agent editing, and project surfaces work cleanly on phones; the Feed commit grid is also hidden when there is no activity.

Added mobile runtime plumbing and broad regression coverage: new host-config, native-runtime, preload-recovery, and service-worker registration utilities are tested, the mobile shell and organization view get unit tests, and Playwright e2e gains an authenticated-app helper plus updated PWA-mobile and responsive specs.
Refreshed mobile release docs (rubric, UI release gate, release workflows, README) to match the new mobile flows.
Eliminated the residual login-panel flash for authenticated users by short-circuiting /login at the route level via a LoginRoute wrapper that calls Navigate to / when a session is already cached.

entries.title: New agents auto-bind to a Home project so first chat persists
entries.summary: Generalized the CEO's Home-project bootstrap so every newly created agent is immediately chat-ready, with self-healing for legacy orphans.

items.text: Generalized the CEO's ensure-home-project helper into a shared home_project module and call it both on POST /api/agents and lazily during chat persistence setup, so new and existing orphan agents get a Home project binding and their first chat turn no longer hard-fails. Existing CEO Home projects are reused via the legacy [aura:ceo-home] marker.

entries.title: Mobile validation hardening, login-flash root fix, and changelog retries
entries.summary: Tightened up the merged mobile work, replaced layered login guards with a single boot-time gate, and hardened changelog generation against transient Anthropic failures.

items.text: Hardened the merged mobile parity work with additional MobileShell, MobileTopbar, AgentEditorForm, and FeedMainPanel tweaks and tests, plus updated layout-capability and responsive-unification e2e coverage and refreshed mobile release-rubric / UI gate docs.

Eliminated the login-form flash at the root by holding the entire route tree until restoreSession resolves: App now returns null while hasResolvedInitialSession is false, producing a brief black screen and then the final view instead of a flash.
Hardened the daily changelog generator with retry/backoff on Anthropic failures so transient errors no longer fail the publish job.
Refreshed the desktop visual audit Playwright spec.

entries.title: Refuse loopback control-plane URL on remote harness deployments
entries.summary: Closes the last remote-deployment hole where cross-agent tool endpoints could silently fall back to 127.0.0.1.

items.text: Cross-agent tool endpoints stamped for a swarm/remote harness now refuse the http://127.0.0.1 fallback when AURA_SERVER_BASE_URL is unset, surfacing a named error at session-init and diagnostic time and emitting a boot-time warning (fatal under AURA_STRICT_CONFIG=1) when the deployment looks remote but the variable is missing.

highlights: Unified Agent model replaces the super-agent path
New in-app Chromium browser in the projects sidekick
Desktop login-flash and update reliability fixes
